memberdef.argsstring: (XDpPsu *InstancePtr, UINTPTR BaseAddress, INTC *IntcPtr, u16 IntrId, u16 DpIntrId, XDpPsu_HpdEventHandler HpdEventHandler, XDpPsu_HpdPulseHandler HpdPulseHandler)
memberdef.name: DpPsu_IntrExample

briefdescription.para: The main entry point for the interrupt example using the XDpPsu driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function will set up the system with interrupts and set up the Hot-Plug-Event (HPD) handlers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is the unique device ID of the DisplayPort TX core instance.
is a pointer to the interrupt instance.
is the unique device ID of the interrupt controller.
is the interrupt ID of the DisplayPort TX connection to the interrupt controller.
is a pointer to the handler called when an HPD event occurs.
is a pointer to the handler called when an HPD pulse occurs.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the system setup failed.
XST_SUCCESS should never return since this function, if setup was successful, is blocking.

simplesect.para: If system setup was successful, this function is blocking in order to illustrate interrupt handling taking place for HPD events.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDpPsu *InstancePtr, INTC *IntcPtr, u16 IntrId, u16 DpIntrId, XDpPsu_HpdEventHandler HpdEventHandler, XDpPsu_HpdPulseHandler HpdPulseHandler)
memberdef.name: DpPsu_SetupInterruptHandler

briefdescription.para: This function sets up the interrupt system such that interrupts caused by Hot-Plug-Detect (HPD) events and pulses are handled.

detaileddescription.para: This function is application-specific for systems that have an interrupt controller connected to the processor. The user should modify this function to fit the application.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is a pointer to the interrupt instance.
is the unique device ID of the interrupt controller.
is the interrupt ID of the DisplayPort TX connection to the interrupt controller.
is a pointer to the handler called when an HPD event occurs.
is a pointer to the handler called when an HPD pulse occurs.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the interrupt system was successfully set up.

simplesect.para: An interrupt controller must be present in the system, connected to the processor and the DisplayPort TX core.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a Hot-Plug-Detect (HPD) event is received by the DisplayPort TX core.

detaileddescription.para: The XDPPSU_INTERRUPT_STATUS_HPD_EVENT_MASK bit of the core's XDPPSU_INTERRUPT_STATUS register indicates that an HPD event has occurred.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

simplesect.para: Use the XDpPsu_SetHpdEventHandler driver function to set this function as the handler for HPD pulses.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a Hot-Plug-Detect (HPD) pulse is received by the DisplayPort TX core.

detaileddescription.para: The XDPPSU_INTERRUPT_STATUS_HPD_PULSE_DETECTED_MASK bit of the core's XDPPSU_INTERRUPT_STATUS register indicates that an HPD event has occurred.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

simplesect.para: Use the XDpPsu_SetHpdPulseHandler driver function to set this function as the handler for HPD pulses.

briefdescription.para: This function is the main function of the XDpPsu interrupt example.

detaileddescription.para: If the DpPsuIntrExample function, which sets up the system succeeds, this function will wait for interrupts. Once a connection event or pulse is detected, link training will commence (if needed) and a video stream will start being sent over the main link.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the interrupt example was unsuccessful - system setup failed.

simplesect.para: Unless setup failed, main will never return since DpPsuIntrExample is blocking (it is waiting on interrupts for Hot-Plug-Detect (HPD) events.

briefdescription.para: Contains a design example using the XDpPsu driver with interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: Upon Hot- Plug-Detect (HPD - DisplayPort cable is plugged/unplugged or the monitor is turned on/off), the main link will be trained.

simplesect.para: This example requires an interrupt controller connected to the processor and the DisplayPort TX core in the system.
For this example to display output, the user will need to select the Test Pattern Generator or initialize and select an appropriate source.
The functions DpPsu_PlatformInit and DpPsu_StreamSrc* are declared extern in xdppsu_common_example.h and are left up to the user to implement.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 aad 09/04/17 Initial creation.
